headline.purpose: Page titles and major narrative turns.

title.purpose: Film titles, modal headings, and product sections.

body.purpose: Marketing and product copy up to 70 characters per line.

shadows.name: poster-lift
shadows.purpose: A film poster under direct hover or keyboard focus.

motion.purpose: One-time scene entrances and dialog transitions.

components.description: The next clear action on a screen.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A contained action that supports rather than competes.

components.name: Text Field
components.description: A labeled field with clear focus and readable placeholder text.

components.name: Mood Chip
components.description: A single short emotion choice, shown here selected.

components.name: Film Poster
components.description: Artwork remains the container; title and metadata sit beneath it.

components.name: Navigation
components.description: A quiet frame with one active route indicator.

narrative.overview: EmotionFlix should feel like choosing a film after the room has gone quiet. The interface recedes, the artwork becomes luminous, and one sharp signal color marks the next action. It is cinematic because it understands framing, pace, and contrast, not because it copies a streaming service. The system is dark because the primary scene is a person browsing film art in a low-light room. It is restrained, not monochrome. Emotion colors appear only when emotion data is present. Citron identifies action and selection. Movie artwork provides the rest of the palette. It rejects Netflix imitation, AI mood-ring aesthetics, senior-project presentation, generic premium minimalism, template landing pages, and icon-font interfaces.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black framing around high-saturation film artwork
One sharp citron action color, used sparingly
A single warm grotesque family with decisive scale changes
Flat surfaces at rest and depth created through cropping and overlap
Direct, literal copy with almost no decorative labels
Original brand geometry and inline SVG controls

rules.name: The Artwork Owns Color Rule
rules.body: If a screen contains posters or backdrops, no decorative gradient competes with them.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Citron is the only brand accent. Emotion colors describe emotion data and nothing else.

rules.name: The No Purple Fog Rule
rules.body: Purple gradients, ambient neon blobs, and colored glass are prohibited.

rules.name: The Film Title Rule
rules.body: Film titles keep their own shape. Do not uppercase them, letter-space them, or make them compete with scores.

rules.name: The One Display Moment Rule
rules.body: A route gets at most one display-sized line. Product routes may get none.

rules.name: The Flat Until Touched Rule
rules.body: A card or poster does not cast a wide shadow until interaction gives that depth a reason.

rules.name: The Single Edge Rule
rules.body: Use either a visible border or a shadow to separate a surface, never both as decoration.

dos: Do let full-bleed backdrops and 2:3 posters carry the visual drama.
Do reserve Signal Citron for the next clear action, selection, or focus state.
Do use one original brand mark and inline SVG controls with a consistent 1.75px stroke.
Do make the relationship between a mood signal and the reordered catalog visible.
Do keep all product copy direct, specific, and short.
Do provide camera, upload, and manual routes with equal functional weight.
Do show demo@demo.com and demo123! inside sign-in only.
donts: Don't build a Netflix imitation with red-on-black branding, copied navigation, or a familiar streaming shell with a different logo.
Don't use AI mood-ring aesthetics: purple gradients, neon emotion blobs, floating glass cards, glowing brains, or claims that the product understands the user.
Don't use senior-project presentation: feature inventories, architecture explanations, class-project language, or UI copy written to prove technical effort.
Don't use generic premium minimalism: beige editorial pages, display-serif affectation, empty whitespace, or copy that says little beautifully.
Don't use template landing pages: eyebrow labels on every section, identical feature cards, hero metrics, or ornamental motion.
Don't use icon fonts, Font Awesome, emoji, or decorative symbols as interface icons.
Don't pair a 1px border with a wide soft shadow on the same surface.
Don't use gradient text, border radii above 16px on surfaces, or side-stripe accents.
Don't put demo language anywhere outside sign-in.
